description:
You are a database administrator who wants a tiny, local-only helper to quickly re-format SQL statements while tuning queries.  
    The container you are working in has no internet access and you do **not** have root privileges, but an offline wheel for the
    package you need has already been dropped in your home directory.

    Objective
    =========
    1. Install *sqlparse* version **0.4.4** for the **current user only**, taking it **exclusively** from the directory
       /home/user/prebuilt_wheels.  
       (Tip: look at the options “--user”, “--no-index”, and “--find-links” offered by *pip*.)

    2. Verify the installation by running a one-liner that pushes the literal string  
          SELECT id, name FROM employees WHERE active = 1;  
       through *sqlparse.format* with the following arguments:  

          keyword_case="upper", reindent=True  

       Redirect the formatted output to the file  
          /home/user/formatted_query.sql  

    3. Create a **two-line** plain-text log file called  
          /home/user/sqlparse_install.log  

       The log format **must be exact** (the automated grader is case- and whitespace-sensitive):

          Line 1 →  sqlparse version: X.Y.Z  
                     (replace **X.Y.Z** with the version reported by *sqlparse.__version__*)  

          Line 2 →  formatted file check: OK  

       • The second line must read **OK** only if  
         – /home/user/formatted_query.sql exists,  
         – it contains at least one newline character, **and**  
         – the keyword **SELECT** appears exactly once in the file.  
       • If any of those conditions fail, write **FAIL** instead of **OK**.  

What will be graded
===================
1. Presence of the sqlparse wheel (0.4.4) in the user site-packages directory created by *pip*.
2. Correct formatting of /home/user/formatted_query.sql (upper-cased keywords plus re-indentation, i.e. the file is multi-line).
3. Exact two-line content of /home/user/sqlparse_install.log as described above.
4. No other files outside the user’s home directory are modified.

You should be able to accomplish everything with just 2–3 terminal commands (install, run formatting, write/check log).
truth:
<!--
      Files/directories that already exist before the agent starts
    -->
    1. Directory /home/user/prebuilt_wheels is present and readable.
    2. Inside it there is a single wheel file whose exact name is
         sqlparse-0.4.4-py3-none-any.whl
       (SHA256: 98fe9baf95142e5e0d6d9a2bf5e641249881fb741ab1b8475d02130eb5c4619d).

    <!--
      Files/directories that MUST exist after the task is done
    -->
    A. /home/user/.local/lib/python*/site-packages/sqlparse/__init__.py  
       (any valid site-packages path accepted; version in sqlparse/__init__.py must be "0.4.4").

    B. /home/user/formatted_query.sql  
       Expected content (exact spacing and newline positions not enforced, but **must** be multiple lines and contain the single
       upper-cased keyword "SELECT"):
          SELECT
              id,
              name
          FROM
              employees
          WHERE
              active = 1;

    C. /home/user/sqlparse_install.log  
       Exact two lines (newline between them, no trailing spaces):
          sqlparse version: 0.4.4
          formatted file check: OK
name: task_000000_3539983e
